name: Montana Capital Car Title Loans
description_short: Montana Capital is a car title loan lender operating since 2007, offering same-day loans from $100 to $50,000 using your vehicle as collateral — no credit check required.
description_long:
Montana Capital Car Title Loans (branded as MontanaCapital®) has operated as a title loan agency since 2007 and claims to have helped more than 375,000 borrowers access emergency cash through vehicle-collateralized lending. The company offers both online and in-person application channels, with 24/7 digital access, and positions itself as a reliable alternative for consumers who cannot qualify for conventional bank loans or credit union financing.

The company's sole product is a car title loan, in which the borrower's vehicle title serves as collateral while the borrower retains possession of and continues driving the vehicle throughout the repayment period. Loan amounts range from $100 to $50,000, with eligibility determined by the applicant's income and the vehicle's equity rather than credit history. Applicants can complete a free online pre-approval in under five minutes with no credit score impact, and approved borrowers can receive funds within 24 hours — either picked up as a Bank of America check at a branch location or via direct deposit. The company can be reached at 1-888-508-5366 during business hours.

Montana Capital distinguishes itself by explicitly accepting borrowers with bad credit, no credit, or prior bankruptcies — a population largely excluded from traditional lending. The company holds a 4.5-star Google rating, with customers consistently citing fast processing times, courteous staff, clear explanation of loan terms, and proactive courtesy text reminders for repayments. The 24/7 availability of the online application and the ability to keep using one's vehicle during the loan term are practical advantages over pawn-based collateral options where the asset must be surrendered.

Consumers should approach Montana Capital with clear-eyed caution. The website does not disclose APR rates or fee structures anywhere on the public-facing pages — a significant transparency gap. Car title loans as a product category typically carry very high interest rates, often in triple-digit annualized APR territory, making them among the most expensive forms of emergency credit available. Defaulting on a title loan carries the real risk of vehicle repossession, which can compound a borrower's financial hardship. One customer review also notes that funds are issued as a Bank of America check rather than cash, which may require an additional step to access. Montana Capital is best treated as a last-resort emergency option when all lower-cost alternatives — credit union PALs, personal loans, employer advances — have been exhausted.
pros: No credit check required — explicitly accepts bad credit and prior bankruptcies
Borrower keeps driving their vehicle throughout the entire loan term
Free pre-approval in under 5 minutes with zero credit score impact
Loan amounts from $100 up to $50,000, sized to vehicle equity and income
Same-day or next-day funding available via branch pickup or direct deposit
24/7 online application access with phone support at 1-888-508-5366
Established since 2007 with 375,000+ borrowers served — long operating history
cons: APR, interest rates, and fee structure are not disclosed anywhere on the public website
Vehicle repossession is a real consequence of default — a significant downside risk
Funds are disbursed as a Bank of America check, not cash — requires an extra step to access
Only 59 Google reviews for a company claiming 375,000+ customers — thin verifiable review base
Title loans are categorically high-cost debt; no rate comparison tools or cost transparency provided
best_for: Car owners with bad credit or past bankruptcy who need emergency cash quickly
Borrowers who have been rejected by traditional banks and credit unions
People who need access to larger emergency amounts (up to $50,000) against vehicle equity
Those who cannot surrender their vehicle and need to keep driving while repaying
diagnosis: Montana Capital is a fit for car owners with bad credit or bankruptcies who need fast emergency cash and cannot qualify for lower-cost alternatives. The main caveat is significant: the website discloses no APR or rate information, and title loans as a category carry high interest rates plus the risk of vehicle repossession on default — borrowers should exhaust credit union PALs and personal loan options first.
services: In-person title loan pickup at branch locations
Direct deposit funding to bank account
Free online pre-approval (no credit score impact)
Title loans for bad credit borrowers
Title loans for borrowers with prior bankruptcies
Loans from $2,000 to $50,000 (larger vehicle equity range)
